title: Does having filler cause the skin to go loose over time？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: Does having filler cause the skin to go loose over time？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: So really common question patients will ask is does having a filler treatment make skin go loose over time [Music] so for most people with a moderate amount of dermal filler added the answer is no however it is theoretically possible if you were to do a rather extreme treatment t…

segments.text: so really common question patients will ask is does having a filler treatment make skin go loose over time [Music] so for most people with a moderate amount of dermal filler added the answer is no however it is theoretically possible if you were to do a rather extreme treatment that you would effectively stretch the tissue to the point where when that filler wore out you would still be able to see that there's almost more skin laxity than before but i think this is really on the far end of cases because most of our treatments are simply not that extreme you can actually see the face and feel it and it doesn't feel taut there's no tension it's mostly that we're replacing the natural volume or filling a potential space and so for that reason although theoretically you might think it could be playing a role i suspect it's so tiny a difference that the answer is no it effectively doesn't stretch your skin but with a small caveat that there might be those one percent treatments where very rarely clinicians have gone you know or other injectors have injected so

much that possibly you could stretch the skin a little bit i think it's kind of with what i call circus treatments really extreme huge lips those types of treatments might pose a risk but for most normal clients normal patients wanting a little bit of beautification or restoration it's not a significant risk at all

transcript: so really common question patients will ask is does having a filler treatment make skin go loose over time [Music] so for most people with a moderate amount of dermal filler added the answer is no however it is theoretically possible if you were to do a rather extreme treatment that you would effectively stretch the tissue to the point where when that filler wore out you would still be able to see that there's almost more skin laxity than before but i think this is really on the far end of cases because most of our treatments are simply not that extreme you can actually see the face and feel it and it doesn't feel taut there's no tension it's mostly that we're replacing the natural volume or filling a potential space and so for that reason although theoretically you might think it could be playing a role i suspect it's so tiny a difference that the answer is no it effectively doesn't stretch your skin but with a small caveat that there might be those one percent treatments where very rarely clinicians have gone you know or other injectors have injected so much that possibly you could stretch the skin a little bit i think it's kind of with what i call circus treatments really extreme huge lips those types of treatments might pose a risk but for most normal clients normal patients wanting a little bit of beautification or restoration it's not a significant risk at all
